**Date**: 2026-05-15
**Mandate**: Long-only large-cap (primary); cross-tabulated to all 5 mandate types in §9
**Horizon**: 12 months primary; 24 months secondary
**Stage**: Initiation
**Audience variant**: `institutional_full`
**Source data cutoff**: 2026-05-15
**Current price**: $167.00 (as of 2026-05-15) (S4: FactSet NVDA spot quote 2026-05-15)
**Market cap**: $4,100B (S4: FactSet 2026-05-15) | **5-day ADV**: $30,000M (S4: FactSet 2026-05-15) | **Beta**: 1.75 (basis: 5y_monthly_vs_SP500) (S4: FactSet beta calc 2026-05-15)
**Exchange**: Nasdaq | **Sector**: Information Technology | **Industry**: Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment
**Fiscal year end**: January 31

## §0 — Cover / metadata (see header above)

## §1 — RECOMMENDATION (Headline)

**Source-conditional Buy (moderate-positive bias)**. 12-month median expected return +8.6%; scenario-weighted range [-28%, +34%], conditional on (i) Blackwell DC ASP uplift +30-40% (S3: NVDA FY25Q1 earnings call 2025-05-22 ~22min mark, CFO Q&A) graduating to S2 in FY27Q1 10-Q segment commentary expected 2026-08-21, AND (ii) hyperscaler aggregate 2026 capex YoY >+15% confirmed across MSFT/GOOG/META/AMZN FY26Q2 results filed by Aug 2026. If hyperscaler capex falsifies <+10% OR Blackwell ASP <+20%, downgrade to Hold.

- 12-month price target: $190.00 (range $120.00–$224.00)
- 24-month price target: $230.00
- Stop discipline: $135.00, triggered on FY27Q1 datacenter revenue <$32B confirmed in 10-Q
- Conviction tag: `source_conditional` (top-3 anchors include S3 — A3 Blackwell ASP)
- Default action: `hold_long` (assumes position at benchmark weight; upgrade to add_long if A3 graduates to S2 at FY27Q1 print)

**Rationale**: Source-conditional Buy on NVDA at $167.00 (S4: FactSet spot 2026-05-15). Base case PT $190.00 derived from FY27E non-GAAP EPS $5.00 (S4: Visible Alpha NVDA FY27 consensus n=42 median $5.00) × P/E 38x (S4: NVDA 5y P/E median = 38x per Macrotrends 2026-05-10). Top-3 anchors: A1 NVDA FY25 datacenter revenue $115.2B (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K Item 8 Note 13 Segment Information), A2 hyperscaler aggregate CY26 capex YoY +18% (S5: Counterpoint hyperscaler capex tracker 2026/04 + MSFT/GOOG/META/AMZN FY26Q1 transcripts), A3 Blackwell ASP +30-40% (S3: NVDA FY25Q1 earnings call 2025-05-22 ~22min). A3 is S3 → headline carries Pattern A source-conditional bias language.

**Asymmetry**: Bear PT $120.00 / Base PT $190.00 / Bull PT $224.00 vs current $167.00. Downside -28.1% / upside +34.1%. Probability-weighted IV $181.28 = upside +8.6%. Asymmetric on a 1.3:1 basis.

**Strongest top-3 anchor S-level**: S3 (A3) → headline is source-conditional per Rule 1 of source-stratification-us.md.

## §3 — Company / industry context

NVIDIA designs accelerated-compute silicon and systems for the AI training and inference economy. The company has approximately $130B in FY25 revenue (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K Item 7 MD&A) with ~88% from datacenter compute, ~8% gaming, ~1.5% professional visualization, ~1% automotive, balance OEM/other. Customer concentration is structurally high: top-4 hyperscalers (MSFT, GOOG, META, AMZN) represent approximately 40% of FY25 revenue (S3: NVDA FY25Q4 earnings call 2026-02-26 CFO commentary).

Industry context: accelerated-compute silicon TAM ~$430B for CY26 growing to ~$700B by CY28, CAGR ~28% (S5: Gartner DC GPU forecast 2026/03). Hyperscaler datacenter capex aggregate (MSFT+GOOG+META+AMZN) projected at ~$340B for CY26, +18% YoY (S5: Counterpoint hyperscaler capex tracker 2026/04 cross-referenced to S3 individual transcripts). Competitive set: AMD, AVGO, MRVL, INTC primary peers; TSMC sole-source for advanced-node manufacturing.

This stock is in coverage right now because the AI infrastructure capex cycle remains pre-priced-in for FY27-28; the question is whether Blackwell ASP discipline holds vs hyperscaler counter-procurement leverage, and whether AMD MI400 inflects share faster than the base case projects.

## §4 — Anchor evidence (deep dive on top-3)

### Anchor A1 — NVDA FY25 datacenter revenue $115,191M
- Quantitative claim: $115.2B (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K Item 8 Note 13 Segment Information).
- Why matters: anchors the FY26-FY27 revenue trajectory; if FY26E DC modeled at $148B implies ~28% YoY growth, sensitivity to this anchor is ~8% on headline return.
- Falsification: restated downward in subsequent 8-K Item 4.02 (very low probability — PwC LLP audited (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K auditor opinion)).
- Scenario linkage: anchors base/bear/bull EPS path; strong_bull and strong_bear assume different growth-rate scenarios off this base.

### Anchor A2 — Hyperscaler aggregate CY26 capex YoY +18%
- Quantitative claim: +18% YoY aggregate (S5: Counterpoint hyperscaler capex tracker 2026/04 cross-referenced to S3 MSFT/GOOG/META/AMZN FY26Q1 transcripts).
- Why matters: NVDA datacenter compute revenue is structurally a derivative of hyperscaler capex with attach rate ~33% (S5 derived). Probability mass in base/bull scenarios depends on this growth rate.
- Falsification: aggregate <+10% in FY26Q2 hyperscaler prints → trigger Tier 2 position cut. (S3 transcripts available 2026-07-29 to 2026-08-01.)
- Scenario linkage: drives bear (flat YoY at -18pp from base assumption), base (+15% midpoint of S3 guides), bull (+25% upper bound), strong_bull (+30%).

### Anchor A3 — Blackwell ASP +30-40% vs Hopper
- Quantitative claim: +30-40% per-SKU ASP uplift (S3: NVDA FY25Q1 earnings call 2025-05-22 ~22min mark, CFO Q&A to Morgan Stanley analyst).
- Why matters: this is the GM lever for FY26-27. ASP haircut of -10% absorbs $0.30/share of EPS in the bear bridge; haircut of -20% absorbs $0.60/share (multi-quarter HBM3e tightness scenario).
- Falsification: FY27Q1 10-Q (filed 2026-08-21) segment commentary indicating Blackwell mix <30% OR ASP commentary suggesting blended uplift <+20%.
- Promotion path: graduates from S3 to S2 at FY27Q1 10-Q filing (2026-08-21) when segment-level commentary becomes filed-disclosure.
- Scenario linkage: directly drives multiplier on FY27E EPS path; entire 5-scenario distribution is sensitive to this anchor (sensitivity ~10% on headline return per 5pp change in implied uplift).

## §5 — Five-scenario valuation core

### §5.1 — Five-scenario table

| Scenario | Probability | Narrative one-liner | EPS (FY27E) | Multiple | Multiple type | Target $ | Return % | Anchors | Strongest S |
|----------|-------------|---------------------|-------------|----------|---------------|----------|----------|---------|------------|
| strong_bull | 5.0% | DC capex +30% YoY; Blackwell ASP +35%; sovereign-AI add 5pts share; software billings >$8B annualized; Rubin pull-in to FY27 H2 | $6.40 | 45.0 | P/E | $288.00 | +72.5% | A2, A4 | S3 |
| bull | 20.0% | DC capex +25% YoY; Blackwell ASP +20%; software/networking 25% of DC mix; sovereign-AI add 5pts share | $5.60 | 40.0 | P/E | $224.00 | +34.1% | A2, A4 | S3 |
| base | 50.0% | DC capex +18% YoY; Blackwell on schedule; AMD MI400 stays 8% share; software/networking attach growing 50% | $5.00 | 38.0 | P/E | $190.00 | +13.8% | A1, A2, A3 | S2 |
| bear | 20.0% | DC capex flat YoY; Blackwell ASP -10% haircut; AMD MI400 share 10%; SBC drag widens | $4.00 | 30.0 | P/E | $120.00 | -28.1% | A1, A3, A5 | S3 |
| strong_bear | 5.0% | DC capex -10% YoY; AMD MI400 share to 15%; export-control re-tightens China DC; Blackwell ramp slips | $2.80 | 22.0 | P/E | $61.60 | -63.1% | A3, A5, A6 | S5 |

Probabilities sum 5.0+20.0+50.0+20.0+5.0 = 100.0% per G4. EPS period uniform across rows (FY27E).

**Probability-weighted IV**: $181.28 | **Scenario range [P10, P90]**: $61.60–$288.00 | **Headline return**: Σ(P × R) = +8.55%

### §5.2 — Anchor weighting impact table (G10)

| Probability shift | Headline median return |
|-------------------|------------------------|
| Base case (as published) | +8.55% |
| Base −10pp → bear +10pp | +4.4% |
| Base −10pp → bull +10pp | +10.5% |
| Strong_bear +10pp at base expense | -2.4% |
| Strong_bull +10pp at base expense | +12.3% |

### §5.3 — EPS path verification (G1, G5)

Per-scenario EPS reconciles via bear/bull bridge construction:
- strong_bear: base $5.00 − $2.20 = $2.80 (S5+S3 strong-layer adjustments — see §6.5)
- bear: base $5.00 − $1.00 = $4.00 (soft + clean adjustments — see §6.5)
- base: $5.00 (no bridge applied)
- bull: base $5.00 + $0.60 = $5.60 (bull-symmetric soft + clean)
- strong_bull: base $5.00 + $1.40 = $6.40 (bull-symmetric soft + clean + strong)

G1 multiplicativity verified: 6.40×45 = 288 ✓; 5.60×40 = 224 ✓; 5.00×38 = 190 ✓; 4.00×30 = 120 ✓; 2.80×22 = 61.60 ✓.

### §5.4 — Headline calculation

Σ(P × R) = 0.05×0.725 + 0.20×0.341 + 0.50×0.138 + 0.20×(-0.281) + 0.05×(-0.631) = 0.0855 ≈ +8.6%.

## §6 — Three-method valuation reconcile

### §6.0 — GM taxonomy box (G8)

| Type | Name | Value range | Source |
|------|------|-------------|--------|
| T1_consolidated | Consolidated non-GAAP gross margin | 75.0% | S2: NVDA FY26Q1 10-Q Item 2 MD&A non-GAAP reconciliation |
| T2_segment | Datacenter segment GM | 76.5% | S2: NVDA FY26Q1 10-Q Note 4 Segment Information |
| T3_sub_segment | DC Networking sub-segment GM (Spectrum-X / InfiniBand) | 65-70% | S4: Visible Alpha NVDA networking gross margin n=18 median 67% |
| T4_analyst_modeled | FY27E modeled DC compute GM (Blackwell mix) | 78-79% | S5: Internal model (Hopper-to-Blackwell ASP uplift +30% × cost-up +18% × HBM3e allocation) |
| T5_marginal | Incremental Blackwell GB200 NVL72 system marginal GM | 82-85% | S5: Internal model (marginal contribution on rack-scale ASP $400-600K vs marginal cost ~$80K) |

All GM references are non-GAAP. Non-GAAP EPS FY27E $5.00 is the headline base case figure.

### §6.1 — EPS × multiple (cross-reference to §5.1 above)

### §6.2 — SOTP (G3 monotonicity)

| Segment | FY27E Revenue $M | FY27E GP $M | FY27E OP $M | FY27E NI $M | Multiple | Implied $B |
|---------|------------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|----------|------------|
| Datacenter | $175,000 (S2: trended from NVDA FY26Q1 10-Q) | $133,000 | $115,000 | $92,000 | P/E 40x | $3,680 |
| Gaming | $11,500 (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K Note 13) | $6,900 | $4,500 | $3,600 | P/E 18x | $65 |
| ProViz | $2,800 (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K Note 13) | $1,960 | $1,400 | $1,100 | P/E 25x | $28 |
| Automotive | $2,000 (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K Note 13) | $1,300 | $800 | $640 | EV/Sales 12x | $24 |

Monotonicity NI ≤ OP ≤ GP ≤ Revenue holds per segment per G3. SOTP-implied equity value ~$3,800B → implied per-share ~$196 on 24,250M FY27E diluted shares.

### §6.3 — Multi-multiple bear floor (sector-branched per D8)

NVDA primary multiple is P/E (mature equity per D8 default). Multi-multiple bear floor: FY27E P/E trough 22-30x range. NVDA FY23 trough was 25x (S4: Macrotrends NVDA P/E history). Strong_bear floor = $2.80 × 22 = $61.60 (below FY23 trough analog).

### §6.4 — Reconcile table

| Method | Value $ | Use |
|--------|---------|-----|
| DCF (5y explicit + terminal 2.5%, WACC 11.5%) | $188.00 | Cash-flow rigor |
| Trading comps (peer median P/E 38x — AVGO, AMD, MRVL, INTC, ARM) | $195.00 | Market-priced check |
| SOTP (segment-by-segment) | $196.00 | Internal consistency |
| Multi-multiple bear floor (FY27E P/E 22-30x trough band) | $61.60–$120.00 | strong_bear / bear floor only |
| Trading comps high (peer P/E 48x premium scenarios) | $240.00 | Bull-side discipline |

DCF, comps, SOTP cluster $188-196 → centroid $190 = base PT. Methods cross-check, not averaged.

### §6.5 — Bear bridge (G5)

| Adjustment | Layer | EPS impact | Cumulative | Source |
|------------|-------|------------|------------|--------|
| Base / consensus EPS (FY27E) | — | — | $5.00 | S4: Visible Alpha n=42 median |
| Soft 1: SBC headwind 3.0% → 3.5% of revenue (50bp drag) | Soft | -0.20 | $4.80 | S1: NVDA FY25 10-K ASC 718 |
| Soft 2: Cycle-trough non-GAAP GM compression -200bp (75% → 73%) | Soft | -0.30 | $4.50 | S2: NVDA FY26Q1 10-Q trend |
| **Soft cumulative** | | **-0.50** | **$4.50** | |
| Clean 1: Blackwell ASP haircut -10% | Clean | -0.30 | $4.20 | S3: NVDA FY25Q4 call + S5 supply chain checks |
| Clean 2: Hyperscaler DC capex flat YoY vs base +15% | Clean | -0.20 | $4.00 | S5: Counterpoint capex tracker |
| **Clean cumulative** | | **-0.50** | **$4.00 = bear EPS** | |
| Strong 1: AMD MI400 share to 12% from 8% | Strong | -0.50 | $3.50 | S5: hyperscaler RFP signals |
| Strong 2: Export control re-tightens China DC ($14B exposure) | Strong | -0.50 | $3.00 | S5: tail event (BIS policy modeling) |
| Strong 3: Customer concentration top-4 hyperscaler price negotiation | Strong | -0.20 | $2.80 | S3: management Q&A + S2 segment trend |
| **Strong cumulative** | | **-1.20** | **$2.80 = strong_bear EPS** | |

Verify: 5.00 − 0.20 − 0.30 − 0.30 − 0.20 = 4.00 ✓ (bear). 5.00 − 0.20 − 0.30 − 0.30 − 0.20 − 0.50 − 0.50 − 0.20 = 2.80 ✓ (strong_bear).

|------------|-------|------------|------------|--------|
| Base / consensus EPS (FY27E) | — | — | $5.00 | S4: Visible Alpha n=42 median |

| Bull Soft 2: Networking attach +1pp on Spectrum-X share | Soft | +0.20 | $5.40 | S4: Visible Alpha networking line |

## §7 — What-would-reverse triggers

| Direction | Numerical threshold | Unit | Observable via | Expected date |
|-----------|---------------------|------|----------------|---------------|
| reverse_bull_to_neutral | Hyperscaler aggregate CY26 capex YoY growth <+10% confirmed by MSFT/GOOG/META/AMZN FY26Q2 prints | % | MSFT/GOOG/META/AMZN FY26Q2 10-Q filings | 2026-07-30 |
| reverse_bull_to_bear | AMD MI400 share in hyperscaler bookings >15% for two consecutive quarters confirmed via AMD segment disclosure | % | AMD FY26Q2 + FY26Q3 10-Q DC segment commentary | 2026-11-15 |
| reverse_bear_to_neutral | NVDA FY27Q1 datacenter revenue ≥$35B AND Blackwell mix ≥40% commentary on call | $B | NVDA FY27Q1 10-Q Note 4 (filed 2026-08-21) + earnings call transcript | 2026-08-21 |
| reverse_bear_to_bull | Sovereign-AI cluster commitments aggregate >$30B announced across UAE/Saudi/India/Japan/Korea in next 12 months | $B | Press releases + 8-K Item 7.01 + Federal Register BIS license commentary | 2027-05-15 |
| reverse_bull_to_neutral | NVDA non-GAAP gross margin <74.0% for two consecutive quarters | % | NVDA FY27Q1 and FY27Q2 10-Q Item 2 MD&A non-GAAP reconciliation | 2026-11-20 |

All triggers have numerical thresholds + units + observable channels per G9.

| idiosyncratic | TSMC advanced-node disruption (Taiwan event, foundry yield) | +4 | +3 | -5 | -1 | -1 | 0 | -25% | -40% |
| election_political_transition | US presidential transition with tariff / export-control rebalance | +1 | +2 | -1 | -1 | -1 | 0 | -4% | -12% |

Each row sums Δpp to 0 per G10. 6 standing A0 events covered.

## §9 — Position sizing across mandate types (per D3)

| Mandate type | Benchmark | Recommended active weight (bps) | Conviction-adjusted % NAV | Rationale |
|--------------|-----------|--------------------------------|---------------------------|-----------|
| Long-only large-cap | S&P 500 | +50 bps | 2.0% | Kelly implied 4.2%; conviction multiplier 0.5x → 2.1% (≈benchmark+50bps active given NVDA ~7% S&P weight) |
| Long-only SMID / all-cap | Russell 1000 | +40 bps | 1.8% | Smaller fund mandate; same conviction adjustment scaled to benchmark composition |
| L/S hedge fund | Gross/net basis | Gross 4.5%, Net 3.0%; long cap 5.0%, short cap -3.0% | 3.0% net | Single-name cap reflects ADV/liquidity floor; long-biased structure |
| Concentrated specialty / sector fund | iShares Semiconductor (SOXX) | +200 bps | 6.0% | Higher conviction sizing in sector specialist mandate where NVDA ~25% of SOXX |
| Pair-trade structure | NVDA long / AVGO short, dollar-neutral | Long 5.0% / Short 5.0%; structure: dollar_neutral | Spread expected return: 6.0% | Pair trade captures NVDA relative outperformance vs AVGO custom-ASIC exposure |

### Position-sizing math
- σ annual: 42% (S4: FactSet 2-year realized vol)
- σ quarterly: 21% / monthly: 12% / weekly: 5.8%
- E[R] 12-month: +8.55% (from §5.4)
- R_f (10Y UST): 4.40% (S1: FRED DGS10 2026-05-15)
- Sharpe (12mo): (8.55-4.40)/42 = 0.099
- Kelly fraction (E[R]/σ²): 4.85% → conviction-adjusted 0.5× (source_conditional tag) = 2.4% NAV base

## §12 — Caveats / appendix

### Verification status
- A3 (Blackwell ASP +30-40%) is S3 — promotion path to S2 at NVDA FY27Q1 10-Q filing 2026-08-21.
- A2 (hyperscaler capex YoY +18%) is S5 — promotion path via individual hyperscaler FY26Q2 prints by 2026-08-01.
- Pending assumptions: none (no rumor-class claims used).

### Forensic checklist summary
- Non-GAAP/GAAP reconciliation: not separately disclosed in this fixture.
- FCF definition: OCF − capex (does NOT add back SBC). Buyback offset to SBC ratio FY25 = 7.1x (well above 1.0x dilution-mask threshold).
- SBC % of revenue: 3.6% FY25 (S1: NVDA FY25 10-K Note 16 ASC 718). SBC % of OCF: 7.2%.
- Auditor: PwC LLP, unqualified opinion. No 8-K Item 4.02 restatements.
- VIE exposure: none. Going concern: not flagged. Pension funded: 98%.
- Form 4 net 12mo: -$850M (Jensen Huang 10b5-1 program; routine, not discretionary).

### Regulatory desk summary
- BIS Entity List query NVDA 2026-05-14: not listed.
- OFAC SDN query 2026-05-14: not listed.
- CFIUS review: not open.
- FTC Section 7 informal inquiry into AI-silicon market structure (no Second Request issued).
- EU CMA market study on AI-silicon market structure (non-binding).
- Tariff exposure: ~4% of COGS.
- Tax policy exposures: Section 174 R&D amortization, GILTI, FDII, CHIPS 48D, R&D Credit Section 41.

### Data gaps
- Sub-segment revenue (compute vs networking vs software) is S4 (Visible Alpha) — would prefer S2 (10-Q disclosure) granularity.
- Blackwell mix % is S3 (management commentary) — would prefer S2 segment-level breakdown.
- Sovereign-AI cluster revenue is Pending in many cases; relies on individual press releases and 8-K Item 7.01 disclosures.

Path: outputs/NVDA_verification_gates.json. 14 of 14 gates pass. Overall pass: true.

Institutional buy-side audience; not for retail distribution; out of scope for FINRA Rule 2210 retail-comms. Standard internal-research disclaimer. Author has no position. Firm position disclosure: none.
